title: FTW Global — Field Prediction Probabilities (Zarr)
description:
Per-pixel class probabilities from the [PRUE model](https://huggingface.co/wherobots/prue-pt2), run over the FTW global Sentinel-2 feature mosaics, as a single cloud-native **Zarr V3** datacube. Three softmax bands — `non_field_background`, `field`, `field_boundaries` — at ~10 m (`8.983119e-5°`) on a global EPSG:4326 grid, for two prediction years (2024 and 2025). The cube has dimensions `(time, band, y, x)` = `(2, 3, 1566049, 4007517)`, float32. It shares the exact grid of the `features/zarr` mosaic, so features and predictions are directly stackable (open both with `xarray`/`rasterix`). The field-boundary `vectors` collection and the `confidence` raster are derived from these probabilities.

This is a GeoZarr-style store (CF-1.8, with the emerging Zarr `proj:`/`spatial:` geo-conventions) but **does not yet implement multiscales/overviews, so it is not yet web-viewable** — that work is in progress. For now it is best consumed analytically (xarray/Zarr), not as map tiles. The underlying field predictions are from the PRUE U-Net model (Muhawenayo et al. 2026, arXiv:2603.27101; 76% IoU / 47% object-F1 on the FTW benchmark), trained on the Fields of the World benchmark (data: https://source.coop/kerner-lab/fields-of-the-world) of 70,462 Sentinel-2 samples across 24 countries (Kerner et al. 2025, arXiv:2409.16252).

providers.name: Microsoft AI for Good Research Lab

variables.description: Per-pixel class probability (softmax) from the PRUE model.

sci:citation: Robinson, C., Muhawenayo, G., Khanal, S., Fang, Z., Corley, I., Tárano, A. M., Estes, L., Marcus, J., Jacobs, N., Kerner, H., Becker-Reshef, I., & Lavista Ferres, J. M. (2026). The first global agricultural field boundary map at 10 m resolution. arXiv:2605.11055.

sci:publications.citation: Kerner, H. et al. (2025). Fields of the World: A Machine Learning Benchmark Dataset for Global Agricultural Field Boundary Segmentation. AAAI-2025. arXiv:2409.16252.
Muhawenayo, G. et al. (2026). PRUE: A Practical Recipe for Field Boundary Segmentation at Scale. arXiv:2603.27101.
Corley, I. et al. (2026). Fields of The World: A Field Guide for Extracting Agricultural Field Boundaries. arXiv:2602.08131.
